_comment: Rule config for scripts/docs_lint.py (CI KB-free lane). Severities: 'error' fails the lint, 'warn' prints only, 'off' is documented-but-inactive. The non_negotiables section is the extension point for work item 3b (D2 instruction single-sourcing): 3b adds/canonicalizes entries and flips severity to 'error' — no lint-code change needed.

scan._comment: Default surface = every *.md under the repo except exclude_dirs, plus extra_files. eval/ and kb_build/ are internal harness dirs (their docs legitimately discuss removed/phantom patterns historically); KB/ is a fetched artifact dir; quarantine/ manifests dead code (W2a convention), so its README legitimately names removed patterns.

tool_counts._comment: Ground truth is parsed from the registries (Tool(name=...) literals); docs claims must match. If a wave deliberately changes the tool surface, bump expected_* here AND update every claim_files doc (mirrors tests/acceptance P01b). allowed = {offline, live, offline+live}. Claim lines are lines containing 'tool' plus a context word, so category headers like 'KB tools (10)' don't false-positive.

phantom_patterns.message: phantom console command: pyproject packages=[] installs no CLI shims; docs must say 'python <path>' (purged in c9f8c0d)
config knob for a nonexistent subsystem (removed from Config/search_config.json in c9f8c0d)
cloud embedding provider reference: the story is local-only (provider knobs purged in c9f8c0d)
python-dotenv was dropped; a tiny built-in parser reads Config/.env (c9f8c0d)
root ./.env.template was deleted; the canonical template is Config/.env.template
deleted duplicate config path; canonical is Config/search_config.json
'extended' is internal-only and returns NOT_IMPLEMENTED; docs must not present it as supported

client_config._comment: The venv trap: system 'python' has no deps. Client-config snippets must point at the venv interpreter.
client_config.message: client config uses a bare python command; must be the venv interpreter path (e.g. .venv\Scripts\python.exe)

non_negotiables.note: Currently legitimately restated across expert prompts. 3b single-sources it, then flips this to 'error'.
Duplicated in td_designer/build.md by design today. 3b decides the single source.
3b flips to 'error' after the instruction channel lands.
